text:
There once was a man named Wilfred. He possessed magical slippers that could travel anywhere. One day, Wilfred stumbled upon a forest where everything was backwards. It started with a friendly oak tree talking to him. Trees had conversations. You could walk in a circle forever without returning home, just wandering.

The oak tree invited him back to its heart. Inside, Wilfred discovered he could control time just by laughing. He decided to freeze an entire afternoon. Life in reverse. The flowers bloomed upside down, birds sang backwards, and the squirrels painted. Everyone had on clothes that wouldn't fit right. An old woman chuckled. She told Wilfred a secret: laughter could mend anything.

Wilfred decided to mend his own past mistakes during his reverse day. He fixed everything—the broken sidewalk, the stolen pet bird. He didn't leave. He kept laughing. When time snapped back, the forest hadn’t changed. Except now, animals were helping each other. The old woman thanked
